title: Seismic Images of Accretive and Erosive Subduction Zones from the Chilean Margin
journal: The Andes: Frontiers in Earth Sciences (Springer)
abstract: Modern seismic imaging methods are used to study subduction processes at the South American convergent margin using reflection and wide-angle/refraction data from the SFB267 collaborative project. Two areas of differing character are investigated: an erosive margin in northern Chile (19°–26°S) and an accretionary margin in south-central Chile (36°–40°S). Three main transects provide an overall impression of the internal structure below the Chilean margin.

location.description: Two contrasting segments of the Chilean margin: erosive north (19°–26°S) and accretionary south-central (36°–40°S)

seismic_lines.name: SO104-07 — CINCA95 MCS reflection profile, 22°S, offshore Antofagasta
SO104-13 — CINCA95 MCS reflection profile, 23.25°S, offshore Antofagasta
SPOC (SO161) onshore seismic reflection profile — 38°S, south-central Chile

data.name: CINCA95 and SPOC MCS Profiles — Erosive and Accretionary Chilean Margin
data.description: MCS reflection data from CINCA95 (SO104) and SPOC (SO161) cruises for erosive (19°–26°S) and accretionary (36°–40°S) segments; provides seismic images for comparison of the two margin types. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: CINCA95 and SPOC OBH Wide-Angle Data — Chilean Margin
data.description: Wide-angle OBH refraction data from CINCA95 and SPOC cruises providing velocity models for both the erosive and accretionary Chilean margin segments. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises SO104 and SO244 — north Chile

_data_note: Springer review chapter (2006). Uses SFB267 collaborative project data from CINCA and SPOC cruises. All data at MGDS.

processing.summary: Synthesis chapter presenting seismic images from both reflection (MCS) and wide-angle refraction (OBH) data from the SFB267 collaborative project for two contrasting Chilean margin segments. The erosive north (CINCA95, 19°–26°S) shows velocity models of the margin undergoing subduction erosion; the accretionary south-central (SPOC, 36°–40°S) shows the accretionary prism and wedge structure. No new data processing; uses published results.
workflow: 2. Synthesis and comparison of seismic images
processing.notes: Chapter 7 in Springer 'The Andes: Active Subduction Orogeny' (Oncken et al. 2006). Synthesis of SFB267 project: CINCA (SO104, 1995), SPOC (SO161, 2001) marine + ANCORP/PRECORP/PISCO/ISSA onshore reflection/refraction experiments. Marine data provided by BGR and IFM-GEOMAR. Both prestack Kirchhoff depth migration (CINCA MCS reflection) and wide-angle velocity models presented. Note: Peter Giese (†) deceased.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. CRITICAL: authors completely wrong (was Sallarès+Ranero) → corrected to 18 actual authors led by Sick, Christof (Chapter 7, Springer Andes volume 2006). vessel updated: R/V SONNE (SO104 CINCA95 + SO161 SPOC) confirmed in acknowledgments. sample_rate_ms: 4 → null (pre-populated default, not confirmed for synthesis chapter). seismic_lines updated with named profiles SO104-07, SO104-13, ANCORP, SPOC 38°S. Software: Kirchhoff PSDM (from PhD thesis Sick 2005 and Buske et al. methods). SFB267 project (DFG-funded). 4500 km MCS + 1300 km wide-angle/refraction + 460 km onshore wide-angle in CINCA area.
